title: Pillowy Moroccan Lamb Tagine Bowl
description: A fresh, pillowy Moroccan dish featuring English mustard, an en-croute pastry top, and no refined sugar.
instructions: Rinse the lamb shoulder and pat it dry with paper towels. Season it with salt, black pepper, cumin, coriander, and smoked paprika.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set it aside. Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for another minute.
Return the lamb shoulder to the pot and add enough chicken broth to cover it. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven. Braise the lamb for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until tender and easily shredded with a fork.
While the lamb is cooking, make the pastry. In a bowl, whisk together the flour and salt.
Gradually add the water to the bowl and mix until a dough forms. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough in half and roll out each half into a thin sheet. Place the sheet over the pot, trimming any excess dough from the edges.
Cut a few slits in the top of the pastry to allow steam to escape and brush the pastry with a little water.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
